Community Health Snapshot

We partner with our communities to improve health for all. Our annual impact report tells the story.

Community Health Snapshot

We partner with our communities to improve health for all. Our annual impact report tells the story.

For more than 80 years, Kaiser Permanente has provided high-quality health care and coverage — but we also do so much more. We unite communities and keep our neighbors healthy by taking action when and where it matters most.

In 2024, we provided health coverage to people with low incomes, removed barriers to good health, and strengthened our communities.

1.5 million

people enrolled in no- or low-cost health coverage and services

1,155

housing units built and preserved

21,000

people helped with applying for grocery benefits

Our 2024 Community Health Snapshot is our annual impact report. Read it to learn how we improve communities and help more people live healthier lives.

Download the 2024 report

Connection is everything

In 2024, we strengthened our connections. We linked members to services and built partnerships to advance community health.

 

In our markets

We invested $1.7 billion in the health of Northern California communities.

40,206

hours volunteered by our employees and physicians

334,000

people received financial assistance for medical care

1,639

residents trained in our graduate medical education program

Download the Northern California snapshot

Communities we serve

See how our work is improving health in your community.

Central Valley

Diablo

East Bay

Fresno

Golden Gate

Greater Sacramento

Greater Southern Alameda Area

Napa-Solano

San Mateo

Santa Rosa

South Bay

We invested $1.2 billion in the health of Southern California communities.

2,511

people enrolled in the Community Health Care Program statewide

701

households connected to legal services to prevent evictions

962

schools accessed Thriving Schools resources

Download the Southern California snapshot

In 2024, we invested $109.8 million in the health of Colorado communities.

5,137

people provided access to mental health services

71,000

people connected to food and nutrition support

19

school districts accessed Thriving Schools resources

Download the Colorado snapshot

In 2024, we invested $63.3 million in the health of Georgia communities.

40 candidates

enrolled in the Mental Health Workforce Accelerator

24,000

patients received help paying for health care services

1,053

people got care from our mobile health vehicle

Download the Georgia snapshot

We invested $38 million in the health of Hawaii communities.

$8.7 million

contributed to support Maui’s wildfire recovery

625

people received no-cost health coverage

31

affordable homes built for Maui health care workers

Download the Hawaii snapshot

In 2024, we invested $343 million in the health of Mid-Atlantic states communities.

5,760

hours completed by summer interns in clinical rotations

5,160

people connected to services that help with basic needs

4,964

people received care from our mobile health vehicle

Download the Mid-Atlantic States snapshot

We invested $240 million in the health of Northwest communities.

150

patients received no-cost oral health care

77,000

pounds of produce distributed to rural communities

14,476

hours volunteered by our employees and physicians

Download the Northwest snapshot

In 2024, we invested $110 million in the health of Washington communities.

43,820

people connected to food and nutrition support

399

affordable housing units renovated or built

18,623

youth participated in mental health programs

Download the Washington snapshot
